title: Fix P1: parseTerminalPaneId throws on malformed percent-encoding instead of falling back to switcher
action: Fix the P1 finding from review session review-pbi-025-terminal-detail-url-20260722 (docs/history/pbi-025-terminal-detail-url/reports/review-20260722-findings.md): parseTerminalPaneId (web/src/main.ts) calls decodeURIComponent(match[1]) with no guard, and a malformed percent-encoded segment (e.g. a truncated multibyte escape like '%E4%B8', or a bare '%') throws an uncaught URIError. Because bootstrap() calls parseTerminalPaneId before its own try block, this exception is never caught, so bootstrap() never renders anything -- a permanent blank screen instead of D3's required silent fallback to switcher. Wrap only the decodeURIComponent call in a local try/catch inside parseTerminalPaneId and return null on any decode failure -- the exact same return value already used for a non-matching path or an otherwise-unresolved pane_id, so the existing D3 silent-switcher path handles it with no other code changes needed. Add a regression test in web/test/main.test.ts's existing parseTerminalPaneId describe block asserting a malformed percent-encoded path (e.g. '/terminal/%' or '/terminal/%E4%B8') returns null instead of throwing.

truths: parseTerminalPaneId('/terminal/%') returns null instead of throwing a URIError.
parseTerminalPaneId('/terminal/%E4%B8') (a malformed multibyte escape) returns null instead of throwing.
bootstrap()'s existing call site and control flow are otherwise unchanged -- the fix is contained entirely inside parseTerminalPaneId.

artifacts.substantive: parseTerminalPaneId wraps its decodeURIComponent call in a try/catch returning null on failure -- no other function changed
new test case(s) in the parseTerminalPaneId describe block proving a malformed percent-encoded path returns null instead of throwing

key_links: bootstrap() still calls parseTerminalPaneId exactly as before -- no call-site change needed since null already flows through the existing D3 fallback
prohibitions: Do not touch the P2/P3 findings from the same review session (logout history entry, deep-link Back-has-no-switcher-beneath, event.state validation, import-time side effects) -- those are separate, non-blocking, and out of scope for this cell.

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Wrapped decodeURIComponent in parseTerminalPaneId (web/src/main.ts) in a local try/catch returning null on decode failure, matching the existing D3 silent-switcher fallback; added a regression test in web/test/main.test.ts's parseTerminalPaneId describe block.
